channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Shortlisted for an Oscar, 'Homebound' is a daring movie about two dear friends
item.description: The movie, now streaming on Netflix, defied current trends in Indian cinema to tell the true story of a friendship between a Muslim and a Hindu Dalit. Martin Scorsese was secretly involved.
item.content:encoded:
The movie, now streaming on Netflix, defied current trends in Indian cinema to tell the true story of a friendship between a Muslim and a Hindu Dalit. Martin Scorsese was secretly involved.

(Image credit: Diaa Hadid/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Five things to know about Hungary's election
item.description: Hungary votes Sunday in a pivotal test of Viktor Orbán's "illiberal democracy," as challenger Péter Magyar taps voter frustration, with stakes for Europe, NATO and the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
Hungary votes Sunday in a pivotal test of Viktor Orbán's "illiberal democracy," as challenger Péter Magyar taps voter frustration, with stakes for Europe, NATO and the U.S.

(Image credit: Pool/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: VP Vance to lead U.S. team in Iran peace talks. And, Artemis II to return to Earth
item.description: Vice President Vance is heading to Pakistan to lead a U.S. team in talks aimed at ending the war in Iran. And, Artemis II is set to return to Earth today.
item.content:encoded:
Vice President Vance is heading to Pakistan to lead a U.S. team in talks aimed at ending the war in Iran. And, Artemis II is set to return to Earth today.

(Image credit: Pool)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: China's Xi meets Taiwan opposition leader ahead of key summit with Trump
item.description: Xi Jinping and the KMT's Cheng Li-wun agreed to pursue peace, but Taiwan's ruling party worries it will enable Beijing to undermine its democracy.
item.content:encoded:
Xi Jinping and the KMT's Cheng Li-wun agreed to pursue peace, but Taiwan's ruling party worries it will enable Beijing to undermine its democracy.

(Image credit: Xie Huanchi)
item.dc:creator: Ashish Valentine

item.title: Iran delegates arrive in Islamabad for talks with U.S., as ceasefire terms remain in dispute
item.description: The fragile ceasefire agreement was tested again on Friday after Iran refused to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Israel and Hezbollah traded strikes in Lebanon, and Kuwait was attacked with drones.
item.content:encoded:
The fragile ceasefire agreement was tested again on Friday after Iran refused to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Israel and Hezbollah traded strikes in Lebanon, and Kuwait was attacked with drones.

(Image credit: Abbas Fakih)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Vance heads to Pakistan for talks to end the war between Iran, the US and Israel
item.description: Vice President JD Vance enters his highest-profile moment Friday as lead U.S. negotiator in face-to-face talks with Iran.
item.content:encoded: Vice President JD Vance enters his highest-profile moment Friday as lead U.S. negotiator in face-to-face talks with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: India cracks down on social media jokes criticizing prime minister
item.description: As criticism of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's response to the Iran war grows, government censors are scrambling to knock down memes that use the Indian leader as the punchline.
item.content:encoded: As criticism of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's response to the Iran war grows, government censors are scrambling to knock down memes that use the Indian leader as the punchline.
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar

item.title: How governments have tried to hide information about the Iran war online
item.description: Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.
item.content:encoded:
Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.

(Image credit: Planet)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: War in Iran strands tons of tea in Kenyan port
item.description: Iran war-linked shipping disruptions have stranded 8 million kg of tea in the Kenyan port of Mombasa, costing the industry about $8 million a week in mounting losses.
item.content:encoded: Iran war-linked shipping disruptions have stranded 8 million kg of tea in the Kenyan port of Mombasa, costing the industry about $8 million a week in mounting losses.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kaloki

item.title: Israel and Lebanon to hold ceasefire talks
item.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to hold direct ceasefire talks for the first time in decades in a bid to halt the violence in southern Lebanon.
item.content:encoded: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to hold direct ceasefire talks for the first time in decades in a bid to halt the violence in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn
